claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children you're responsible for? If yes, could you tell me how many children you have and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have two - Alex who is 8, and Blake who is 20.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, I need to gather some more details.

Let me start with Blake, who is 20. Is Blake currently in approved education or training? For example, is Blake enrolled in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent) or an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. They've finished school and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know about Blake.

Now, let me ask about your living situation. Do both Alex and Blake live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Now I need to understand your responsibility for the children. Are you the parent of Alex and Blake, or do you have another relationship to them (such as being their legal guardian or carer)?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you currently living in the UK, and do Alex and Blake also normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, no - I don't live in the UK. We live abroad currently.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's an important detail.

Could you tell me which country you're currently living in? Also, are you or your partner receiving any UK benefits, or do you have any connection to the UK such as working for a UK-based employer or being a Crown servant?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: We're living in Spain at the moment. And no, we're not receiving any UK benefits or working for a UK employer - no Crown service or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you an accurate assessment of your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government guidance, I can now provide you with an assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake. Here's why:

**For Alex (8 years old):**
- While Alex meets the age requirement (under 16), you do not meet the residency requirement. Child Benefit requires that you live in the UK, unless you're a Crown servant or working for a UK-based employer. Since you're living in Spain without any of these qualifying UK connections, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex.

**For Blake (20 years old):**
- Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria because they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Additionally, the residency requirement issue applies to Blake as well.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK or if Blake were to enter approved education), your eligibility could be reassessed.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so they meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. However, according to UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child AND you live in the UK. The guidance states you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. Since you are living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, not working for a UK-based employer, and not receiving any UK benefits, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old. Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training. Since Blake is not currently in any education or training, they do not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit. Additionally, you are living in Spain and are not a Crown servant or working for a UK-based employer, so you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Blake either.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a parent of two children - Alex (8) and Blake (20). While Alex meets the age requirement, the family does not meet the residency requirement as they currently live in Spain with no qualifying UK connection (such as Crown service or UK employment). Blake does not meet the age/education requirement as they are 20 and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.

The guidance states you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. Since you are living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, not working for a UK-based employer, and not receiving any UK benefits, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old. Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training. Since Blake is not currently in any education or training, they do not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit. Additionally, you are living in Spain and are not a Crown servant or working for a UK-based employer, so you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Blake either.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a parent of two children - Alex (8) and Blake (20). While Alex meets the age requirement, the family does not meet the residency requirement as they currently live in Spain with no qualifying UK connection (such as Crown service or UK employment). Blake does not meet the age/education requirement as they are 20 and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
